_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Surgical debridement in ulcers associated with necrotic tissue or slough to remove dead (necrotic) tissue or foreign material from and around a wound to expose healthy tissue.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication of procedure and planned line of management
Clinical Photographs of the affected part

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned lined of treatment, indication for procedure?
Did clinical presentation confirm the diagnosis?
Was clinical presentation/assessment ± photograph indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the clinical evaluation ± photograph indicative of procedure?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing or lacking details on history, signs, symptoms, planned treatment, and indication for procedure.
Clinical photograph of affected part missing or insufficient to assess condition.
Clinical presentation/assessment (or photograph) not indicative of procedure.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) missing or lacking daily vitals and treatment details.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes missing or incomplete.
Detailed discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice.

clinical_key_pointers: Wounds can be classified as acute or chronic (present for at least 6 weeks), and further as arterial, venous, trophic, malignant, etc.
An ulcer is a discontinuity of an epithelial surface.
Debridement is the process of removing dead (necrotic) tissue or foreign material from and around a wound to expose healthy tissue.
surgical_options: Surgical debridement in ulcers associated with necrotic tissue or slough.
post_op_care: Clean the wound with physiological normal saline or tapwater only (antiseptics delay wound healing).
Daily dressing: Gauze adheres to the woundbed and it may remove viable tissue from the wound surface on removal, resulting in delayed wound healing.
Occlusive (moisture retentive) dressings (Hydrocolloid gel) for clean and shallow ulcers without pus discharge or infection.
Calcium alginate dressing for bleeding wounds and wounds with a cavity.
Encourage daily bath; avoid walking bare foot or using slippers with interdigital bars.
Reduce standing or excessive walking; wear elastic stockings.
Elevation of leg and foot end of the bed while sleeping and leg exercises to activate the calf muscle pump in leg ulcers.
comorbidity_management: Identify and treat predisposing factors such as Diabetes mellitus, Peripheral arterial or venous disease, Severe anaemia, Protein deficiency, Rheumatoid arthritis, Systemic vasculitis, Cushing's syndrome, Systemic steroid therapy.
Identify microorganism and treat accordingly; send pus/discharge for culture and sensitivity.
Exhibit appropriate antibiotics; Tubercular ulcer is treated with antitubercular drug for a minimum period of 6 months.
